perturbation.original_text: Section 6.1 of the Agreement shall be, and hereby is, amended by deleting the date "September 30, 2003" where it appears therein, and inserting in its place the date "July 20, 2006".
perturbation.changed_text: Section 6.1 of the Agreement shall be, and hereby is, amended by deleting the date "September 30, 2003" where it appears therein, and inserting in its place the date "September 30, 2003".
perturbation.explanation: By changing the end date back to the original, it creates a contradiction with the stated intention of the amendment, which was to extend the term of the agreement. This is contradictory to recitals C where it indicates the term will be extended, which creates uncertainty about the actual term and validity of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Partnership, the Partners and Manager desire to amend the Agreement to extend the term of the Agreement and to add a new section concerning curtailment of the LIFO Partners (as defined below), all as set forth in this Amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reverts the amendment's intended extension of the agreement term back to the original date. This directly contradicts the statement in the contradicted text that the agreement is being amended 'to extend the term of the Agreement.' The changed text undermines the explicit purpose of the amendment, creating a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, no change to the Transmission Line shall be made without the prior unanimous consent of the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners), which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, changes to the Transmission Line can be made at the Manager's sole discretion, after informing the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners).
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires unanimous consent of the partners (excluding LIFO Partners) for any changes to the Transmission Line to eliminate curtailment causes, restricting Manager's unilateral action. The altered text allows Manager to make these changes at their sole discretion after merely informing the Partners. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the Manager's authority and Partner's rights concerning Transmission Line modifications, potentially leading to disputes over operational decisions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the LIFO Partners shall inform the Manager in writing of an agreement among them concerning the priority of the curtailment of their respective Projects, the Manager shall effect any curtailment under these provisions according to such priority. If the LIFO Partners fail to so inform the Manager, the Manager shall in its discretion determine which of the Projects of the LIFO Partners to curtail. The Manager shall cooperate with the LIFO Partners to attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment under this provision, provided that any expenses in connection with such cooperation shall be paid by the LIFO Partners. In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, changes to the Transmission Line can be made at the Manager's sole discretion, after informing the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners).
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates unanimous consent from partners (excluding LIFO) for Transmission Line changes related to curtailment, protecting partner rights. The changed text grants the Manager sole discretion after simply informing the partners, directly contradicting the consent requirement. The contradicted text, which is actually the changed text itself, highlights the Manager's power to act unilaterally which contradicts the original need for unanimous partner consent. This creates a direct and significant conflict concerning decision-making authority and partner rights regarding Transmission Line modifications.
